metadata.title: Why the Wave Crests Move Twice as Fast as the Packet

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Jambo, Liam — in for Bear. One packet, two velocities? Can you explain it, Bear?

props.segment: Why the Wave Crests Move Twice

beats.narration_text: Watch a quantum wave packet move. The probability envelope crawls forward — that's the group velocity, where the particle actually is. But the crests inside sprint ahead at twice the speed. One packet, two velocities.

props.segment: Why the Wave Crests Move Twice as Fast as the Packet
props.command: why do wave crests outrun the packet?

props.segment: Why the Wave Crests Move Twice as Fast as the Packet
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: a Gaussian envelope (TEAL) translating right at group velocity v_g while CRIMSON sine crests inside travel at v_ph = v_g/2 — crests born at back, die at front"

beats.narration_text: Watch this quantum wave packet travel. See the ripples inside? They keep racing forward — born at the back, dying at the front.

production_viz.label: wave packet traveling right — crests overtaking envelope
production_viz.mechanic: a Gaussian envelope (TEAL filled area) translates right at speed v_g; a sine-wave pattern (CRIMSON) inside the envelope travels at v_ph = v_g/2 so crests visibly emerge from the left side of the envelope and exit through the right; the envelope center is marked with a dot
production_viz.note: cold open — no labels yet, let the motion speak

props.segment: Why the Wave Crests Move Twice as Fast as the Packet
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: a classical pulse on a string — internal oscillations locked to the envelope, both traveling at the same speed, a dot on one crest moving with the envelope"

beats.narration_text: Classical physics has nothing like this. A pulse on a guitar string moves as one — its wiggles travel with the blob, not through it.

production_viz.label: classical pulse — wiggles locked to envelope
production_viz.mechanic: a hump (SLATE outline) with internal oscillations travels right; a dot on one crest moves exactly with the envelope; no separation between crest speed and envelope speed

beats.narration_text: Classical physics predicts both speeds are identical. A quantum wave packet shows otherwise — the crests are two times faster than the blob they live inside. Why?

card.copy: Why do the crests inside a quantum packet race at twice the speed of the packet itself?

props.segment: Why the Wave Crests Move Twice as Fast as the Packet
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: three TEAL sine waves at slightly different wavelengths stacking and combining into a Gaussian blob — annotate 'many wavelengths added'"

beats.narration_text: A wave packet is not one wave. It's a crowd of pure waves, each with a slightly different wavelength, added up so they cancel everywhere except in one blob.

production_viz.label: superposition of component waves builds the packet
production_viz.mechanic: three sine waves (TEAL, slightly different wavelengths) appear stacked; they then overlay and combine into the TEAL Gaussian blob; annotation: 'many wavelengths added'
production_viz.note: the problem setup: establish the wave packet as a superposition

props.segment: Why the Wave Crests Move Twice as Fast as the Packet
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: three TEAL sine wave segments stacked vertically — top has shortest wavelength with long velocity arrow, bottom has longest wavelength with short arrow, labels 'short wavelength, higher speed' and 'long wavelength, lower speed'"

beats.narration_text: Each component wave travels at its own speed. A wave with shorter wavelength means higher momentum — and in quantum mechanics, higher momentum means moving faster.

production_viz.label: components at different speeds
production_viz.mechanic: three labeled sine wave segments (TEAL) arranged vertically; the top has the shortest wavelength and a longer velocity arrow; the bottom has the longest wavelength and shorter arrow; labels: 'short wavelength, higher speed' top, 'long wavelength, lower speed' bottom
production_viz.note: velocity spread is what causes the crest / envelope speed difference

props.segment: Why the Wave Crests Move Twice as Fast as the Packet
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: a single CRIMSON sine wave traveling right — one crest highlighted with a dot, velocity arrow labeled 'phase velocity', beneath it 'p / 2m' in mono font"

beats.narration_text: The individual wave crests move at what's called the phase velocity — a speed set by the wave's own frequency. For a quantum particle, that turns out to be half the particle's actual speed.

production_viz.label: phase velocity label on single crest
production_viz.mechanic: a single CRIMSON sine wave travels right; one crest is highlighted as a dot; velocity arrow labeled 'phase velocity' tracks the crest; underneath: 'p / 2m' in MONO font
production_viz.note: introduce PHASE VELOCITY with CRIMSON = the crest / phase structure

props.segment: Why the Wave Crests Move Twice as Fast as the Packet
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: TEAL envelope blob with TEAL arrow labeled 'group velocity = p/m' and beside it a CRIMSON arrow half as long labeled 'phase velocity = p/2m' — TEAL arrow exactly twice the CRIMSON one"

beats.narration_text: But the blob — the envelope, the location of the actual particle — moves at the group velocity. That's where the momentum components' averages settle. And that is twice the phase velocity.

production_viz.label: group vs phase velocity arrows
production_viz.mechanic: the TEAL envelope blob sits center; a TEAL arrow labeled 'group velocity = p/m' points right; beside it a shorter CRIMSON arrow labeled 'phase velocity = p/2m' points right; the TEAL arrow is exactly twice as long as the CRIMSON one
production_viz.note: THE key comparison — the ratio is 2:1

props.segment: Why the Wave Crests Move Twice as Fast as the Packet
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: TEAL envelope advancing with CRIMSON crests appearing at the rear edge labeled 'born here', traveling through to the front edge labeled 'die here', dots marking individual crest lifetimes"

beats.narration_text: So the envelope races ahead of its internal crests. New crests keep being born at the trailing edge, push forward, and vanish at the leading edge.

production_viz.label: crests born at back, die at front
production_viz.mechanic: TEAL envelope advancing; CRIMSON crests appear labeled at the rear edge 'born here', travel through to the front edge labeled 'die here'; trail of dots marks individual crest lifetimes inside the packet
production_viz.note: THE MECHANISM beat — the through-traffic of crests

beats.narration_text: This is not a glitch. It's why phase velocity has no physical meaning for a quantum particle. No detector ever records where a crest is — only where the blob is.

props.headline: This is not a glitch. It's why phase velocity has no…

beats.scene_description: a detector screen showing single bright dots appearing in a cluster (the packet envelope has passed), with ghostly wave interference lines fading; no visible individual crest marked
beats.image_prompt: B09, a glowing detector screen showing a cluster of bright pinpoint dot-impacts where a quantum particle landed, surrounded by faint concentric ring interference patterns fading outward, dark laboratory background, flat editorial illustration style on cream ground, no text, no labels

props.segment: Why the Wave Crests Move Twice as Fast as the Packet
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: ocean wave group analogy — TEAL shaded group envelope advancing slowly while CRIMSON crest markers advance faster, small arrows showing both speeds"

beats.narration_text: The same separation appears in all dispersive waves. Ocean swell crests race through the wave group; the group is what carries the energy to shore.

production_viz.label: ocean wave group analogy
production_viz.mechanic: a wavy surface (TEAL fills, SLATE outline) shows three crests in a group; the group envelope (TEAL shaded region) advances slowly right while individual crests (CRIMSON markers) advance faster; small arrows mark both speeds
production_viz.note: implication beat — this is universal in dispersive media

props.segment: Why the Wave Crests Move Twice as Fast as the Packet
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: TEAL envelope with CRIMSON crests inside — two bracketing labels 'particle here' pointing to the envelope and 'phase only' pointing to a crest with a strikethrough line"

beats.narration_text: For quantum mechanics, it means the particle's location is the blob, its velocity is the group velocity, and the internal crests are the phase — carrying no energy, carrying no particle.

production_viz.label: blob = particle, crests = phase only
production_viz.mechanic: a large TEAL envelope with CRIMSON crests inside; two bracketing SerifLabels: 'particle here' pointing to the envelope, 'phase only' pointing to a crest; the crest label has a line through it — excluded from physical meaning
production_viz.note: implication — what the two speeds mean physically

props.segment: Why the Wave Crests Move Twice as Fast as the Packet
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: timeline animation p=1 dimensionless — at t=0 TEAL blob with CRIMSON crests numbered 1-3; at t=1 blob moved 1 unit, crests moved 0.5 units; ruler marks distances; MONO labels show illustrative values"

beats.narration_text: Here's an illustrative case. Take an electron with momentum equal to one unit. Its phase velocity is one-half — crests moving at half speed. Its group velocity is one — the particle moving at full speed. Start the clock: after one second, the blob has traveled one unit. The crests inside it have only traveled half a unit. Every crest that started at the front has passed clean through the blob and come out the back.

production_viz.label: illustrative example: p=1, v_g=1, v_ph=0.5
production_viz.mechanic: timeline animation: at t=0, TEAL blob with CRIMSON crests numbered 1-3; at t=1, blob has moved 1 unit (TEAL), but crests have only moved 0.5 units (CRIMSON); crest #1 which was at front is now at center; ruler marks distances; MONO labels show the illustrative values
production_viz.note: EXAMPLE act — illustrative values labeled as such; p=1 is dimensionless for visual clarity

beats.narration_text: The wave crests move at the phase velocity — half the particle's speed. The packet itself, carrying the actual particle, moves at the group velocity — the particle's real speed.

card.copy: The crests run at half-speed — the packet runs at full speed.

beats.narration_text: The crests and the envelope are always moving at different speeds. Paste this into Claude and mark them both.

props.segment: Why the Wave Crests Move Twice as Fast as the Packet
props.command: claude "animate a quantum wave packet showing phase velocity and group velocity separately — mark the crest positions and the packet envelope to show the factor-of-two difference"

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Here's what the body just demonstrated. The wave crests move at the phase velocity — half the particle's speed. The packet itself, carrying the actual particle, moves at the group velocity — the particle's real speed. The crests and the envelope are always moving at different speeds. Paste this into Claude and mark them both.

props.artifactHeading: Why the Wave Crests Move Twice as Fast
artifactLines: The wave crests move at the phase velocity — half the particle's speed.
The crests and the envelope are always moving at different speeds.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Why the Wave Crests Move Twice as Fast as the Packet] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
